quality.rationale: 基于史瓦西度规利用Killing矢量守恒量联立四速归一化条件，通过链式法则消去固有时推导出轨道微分方程

origin.question: Problem 15.5. Derive a first-order differential equation for the trajectory (r as a function of $\phi$ ) for equatorial orbits in the Schwarzschild geometry.## SolutionSolution 15.5. Choose the equatorial plane to be $\theta = \pi /2$ . Then $\mathbf{u}^{\theta} = 0$ . One first integral of the motion is the normalization of the 4-velocitywhich implies$$\mathrm {g}^{0 0} \mathrm {u}_{0}^{2} + \mathrm {g}^{\phi \phi} \mathrm {u}_{\phi}^{2} + \mathrm {g}_{\mathrm {r r}} \mathrm {u}^{\mathrm {r}}^{2} = - 1. \tag {1}$$From the Killing vectors $\partial/\partial t$ and $\partial/\partial \phi$ two more first integrals are$$u_{\phi} = \tilde {L} = \text {c o n s t a n t}$$$$u_{0} = - \bar {\mathbf {E}} = \text {c o n s t a n t}$$where $\tilde{\mathbf{L}}$ and $\tilde{\mathbf{E}}$ are respectively the angular momentum per unit rest mass and energy per unit rest mass. Equation (1) now implies$$\left(\frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {r}}{\mathrm {d} \tau}\right)^{2} = \frac {- 1 - \mathbf {g}^{0 0} \tilde {\mathbf {E}}^{2} - \mathbf {g}^{\phi \phi} \tilde {\mathbf {L}}^{2}}{\mathbf {g}_{\mathbf {r r}}} \tag {2}$$or with the explicit metric functions:$$\mathrm {d} \mathbf {r} / \mathrm {d} \tau = \pm \left\{\left[ - 1 + \tilde {\mathrm {E}}^{2} \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathrm {m}}{\mathrm {r}}\right)^{- 1} - \frac {\tilde {\mathrm {L}}^{2}}{\mathrm {r}^{2}}\right] \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathrm {m}}{\mathrm {r}}\right)\right\}^{\frac {1}{2}}. \tag {3}$$From $\mathbf{u}^{\phi} = \mathbf {g}^{\phi}\tilde{\mathbf{L}}$ we have $\mathrm{d}\phi /\mathrm{d}\tau = \tilde{\mathrm{L}} /\mathrm{r}^2$ . Combining this with Equation (3) gives us finally$$\frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {r}}{\mathrm {d} \phi} = \pm \frac {\mathbf {r} ^ {2}}{\tilde {\mathbf {L}}} \left\{\left[ - 1 + \tilde {\mathbf {E}} ^ {2} (1 - 2 \mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r}) ^ {- 1} - \frac {\tilde {\mathbf {L}} ^ {2}}{\mathbf {r} ^ {2}} \right] (1 - 2 \mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r}) \right\} ^ {\frac {1}{2}}.
origin.solution: Choose the equatorial plane to be $\theta = \pi /2$ . Then $\mathbf{u}^{\theta} = 0$ . One first integral of the motion is the normalization of the 4-velocitywhich implies$$\mathrm {g}^{0 0} \mathrm {u}_{0}^{2} + \mathrm {g}^{\phi \phi} \mathrm {u}_{\phi}^{2} + \mathrm {g}_{\mathrm {r r}} \mathrm {u}^{\mathrm {r}}^{2} = - 1. \tag {1}$$From the Killing vectors $\partial/\partial t$ and $\partial/\partial \phi$ two more first integrals are$$u_{\phi} = \tilde {L} = \text {c o n s t a n t}$$$$u_{0} = - \bar {\mathbf {E}} = \text {c o n s t a n t}$$where $\tilde{\mathbf{L}}$ and $\tilde{\mathbf{E}}$ are respectively the angular momentum per unit rest mass and energy per unit rest mass. Equation (1) now implies$$\left(\frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {r}}{\mathrm {d} \tau}\right)^{2} = \frac {- 1 - \mathbf {g}^{0 0} \tilde {\mathbf {E}}^{2} - \mathbf {g}^{\phi \phi} \tilde {\mathbf {L}}^{2}}{\mathbf {g}_{\mathbf {r r}}} \tag {2}$$or with the explicit metric functions:$$\mathrm {d} \mathbf {r} / \mathrm {d} \tau = \pm \left\{\left[ - 1 + \tilde {\mathrm {E}}^{2} \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathrm {m}}{\mathrm {r}}\right)^{- 1} - \frac {\tilde {\mathrm {L}}^{2}}{\mathrm {r}^{2}}\right] \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathrm {m}}{\mathrm {r}}\right)\right\}^{\frac {1}{2}}. \tag {3}$$From $\mathbf{u}^{\phi} = \mathbf {g}^{\phi}\tilde{\mathbf{L}}$ we have $\mathrm{d}\phi /\mathrm{d}\tau = \tilde{\mathrm{L}} /\mathrm{r}^2$ . Combining this with Equation (3) gives us finally$$\frac {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {r}}{\mathrm {d} \phi} = \pm \frac {\mathbf {r} ^ {2}}{\tilde {\mathbf {L}}} \left\{\left[ - 1 + \tilde {\mathbf {E}} ^ {2} (1 - 2 \mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r}) ^ {- 1} - \frac {\tilde {\mathbf {L}} ^ {2}}{\mathbf {r} ^ {2}} \right] (1 - 2 \mathbf {M} / \mathbf {r}) \right\} ^ {\frac {1}{2}}.

cached_fact_sheet.kretschmann: \frac{4 \left(M^{2} r^{6} \left(2 M - r\right) - 2 M^{2} r^{5} - M^{2} r^{4} \left(2 M - r\right)^{3} + M^{2} r^{2} \left(- 2 M + r\right) - 2 M^{2} r \left(2 M - r\right)^{4} + M^{2} \left(2 M - r\right)^{3} + r^{3} \left(2 M - r\right)^{4}\right)}{r^{9} \left(2 M - r\right)^{2}}
